description: Granular skill taxonomy for IELTS Academic Reading, derived from official Cambridge IELTS Reading Band Descriptors and question-type performance criteria.

categories.description: Locating and retrieving specific information from texts

skills.description: Accurately locating and extracting specific facts, figures, and details from the text

ranks.1: Frequently cannot locate specific details; answers based on guessing or misreading.
ranks.2: Locates obvious details in straightforward texts but misses details in complex or long passages.
ranks.3: Reliably retrieves explicit details in most texts; occasional errors with dense or technical content.
ranks.4: Consistently retrieves both explicit and implicit details across complex academic texts.
ranks.5: Precisely locates and extracts all required details with full accuracy across all text types.

skills.description: Rapidly reading to identify the main idea or purpose of a passage or paragraph

ranks.1: Reads word-by-word; cannot identify the overall gist of a passage efficiently.
ranks.2: Identifies the general topic but misses the specific purpose or argument of sections.
ranks.3: Identifies the main idea of most paragraphs; occasionally misses the central argument.
ranks.4: Efficiently identifies the main idea, purpose and argument structure across all sections.
ranks.5: Rapidly and accurately identifies gist, purpose and argumentative structure in all text types.

skills.description: Locating specific words, names, numbers or phrases without reading the full text

ranks.1: Cannot scan effectively; reads the entire passage to find specific information.
ranks.2: Scans for exact words but misses synonyms, paraphrases and related expressions.
ranks.3: Scans effectively for keywords and common synonyms in most texts.
ranks.4: Efficiently scans for keywords, synonyms and paraphrased information across all texts.
ranks.5: Expert scanning — instantly locates target information including complex paraphrases.

categories.description: Understanding meaning, argument and writer intent

skills.description: Identifying the central theme or main argument of a passage or section

ranks.1: Confuses specific details with the main idea; cannot identify the central argument.
ranks.2: Identifies the general topic but conflates it with a supporting detail or example.
ranks.3: Correctly identifies the main idea in most texts; occasional confusion in abstract passages.
ranks.4: Consistently identifies the central argument across all text types including academic abstracts.
ranks.5: Precisely identifies the main idea and distinguishes it from supporting points in all texts.

skills.description: Drawing logical conclusions from information that is implied but not explicitly stated

ranks.1: Cannot draw inferences; answers only based on explicitly stated information.
ranks.2: Makes simple inferences in straightforward texts but fails with complex implied meaning.
ranks.3: Draws reasonable inferences in most texts; struggles with abstract or nuanced implication.
ranks.4: Consistently draws accurate inferences including from academic and technical texts.
ranks.5: Expert inference — correctly interprets all implied meaning, tone and unstated conclusions.

skills.description: Correctly distinguishing between information that is true, false, or not given in the text

ranks.1: Confuses False and Not Given consistently; applies own knowledge rather than the text.
ranks.2: Identifies True statements reliably but confuses False with Not Given.
ranks.3: Correctly applies T/F/NG in most cases; occasional errors with borderline Not Given.
ranks.4: Consistently accurate on T/F/NG across complex texts with subtle distinctions.
ranks.5: Perfect T/F/NG accuracy — reliably distinguishes all three categories across all texts.

skills.description: Identifying the writer's purpose, opinion, attitude or degree of certainty

ranks.1: Cannot distinguish facts from opinions; misreads the writer's attitude consistently.
ranks.2: Identifies obvious opinions but misses hedging language, irony or implicit attitude.
ranks.3: Identifies writer's purpose and main attitude in most texts.
ranks.4: Consistently identifies purpose, attitude and degree of certainty across complex texts.
ranks.5: Expert identification of all dimensions of writer's stance including subtle hedging.

categories.description: Understanding and using contextual vocabulary knowledge

skills.description: Deducing the meaning of unfamiliar words from surrounding context

ranks.1: Cannot deduce word meaning from context; relies entirely on prior vocabulary knowledge.
ranks.2: Deduces meaning of simple words from clear context; fails with academic or technical terms.
ranks.3: Reliably deduces meaning of most unfamiliar words from contextual clues.
ranks.4: Consistently deduces precise meaning from context including abstract academic vocabulary.
ranks.5: Expert contextual deduction — accurately infers all word meanings including technical terms.

skills.description: Recognising when a question or heading paraphrases information in the text

ranks.1: Cannot recognise paraphrases; only matches exact words between question and text.
ranks.2: Recognises simple synonyms but misses structural paraphrases and complex rewording.
ranks.3: Recognises most paraphrases including common synonyms and sentence restructuring.
ranks.4: Consistently matches paraphrased questions to text across all complexity levels.
ranks.5: Expert paraphrase recognition — instantly matches all reformulations including complex academic rewording.

categories.description: Understanding how academic texts are organised and developed

skills.description: Identifying the function of individual paragraphs within the overall text structure

ranks.1: Cannot identify paragraph function; treats all paragraphs as containing the same type of information.
ranks.2: Identifies introductions and conclusions but misses the function of body paragraphs.
ranks.3: Correctly identifies the purpose of most paragraphs including examples and counter-arguments.
ranks.4: Consistently maps paragraph function across complex multi-section academic texts.
ranks.5: Expert structural analysis — precisely identifies all paragraph functions and their relationships.
